text:
Binghamton CS discussion (College Confidential), post by swimstar2493 (2011-04-03):
Hello, I am a potential incoming freshman into Binghamton in the fall. I was accepted into the computer science program at the watson school of engineering and sciences and i was wondering if anyone can give me any info on the difficulty of the course, professors, and anything else that can be useful about the program at binghamton.

Binghamton CS discussion (College Confidential), post by Bonacspud (2011-04-03):
My son was too, hoping someone responds. I noticed in comparing colleges that he was accepted to, Binghamton has the most computer programs/majors, numbered in the 80's compared to some schools with only 20+ programs. I believe Bing has about 350 CS undergraduate students.

Binghamton CS discussion (College Confidential), post by Bootes (2011-04-03):
I'm a senior CS major. I think the CS department is pretty good in general. Eileen Head (Undergraduate Program Director) is very helpful and you should definitely get to know her. A good thing about CS is that upper level courses have a class size limit and no CS courses are allowed to be taught by TAs.

Binghamton CS discussion (College Confidential), post by Bootes (2011-04-03):
I guess that classes are difficult, but it's usually material I actually want to learn. I think that's really the most important thing to consider. I've taken a few English courses recently and the work/time commitment required by them is much less than CS courses. But I wouldn't be interested in taking most English courses, and it would be much more difficult to find a job if my degree was in English. You can always take

b if my degree was in English. You can always take CS110/140 (Intro Programming Courses) and see what you think of them. I don't feel that the classes get more difficult than those, you mainly just keep building at a similar pace from there.

Professors to avoid: Foreman and Steflik. Foreman teaches a pass/fail required 2 credit course CS 101. You don't need to/can't avoid him there, but don't take him for a real 3/4 credit course like Operating Systems. Neither teaches anything in lecture and then often

Neither teaches anything in lecture and then often requires pretty difficult projects that you need to completely figure out how to do on your own. You then have no idea what to study for on the tests (because you haven't been learning anything in lecture).

Professors I've liked: Garrison, Govindaraju (Madhu), Greene, Iwobi, Richard Eckert (seems to have retired), Kartik. Greene is difficult and doesn't come off as that friendly, but he teaches you a lot and I've heard he's very helpful in office hours if

nd I've heard he's very helpful in office hours if you need help. Garrison teaches 373 which is unlike any other course you'll take and I don't think anyone could teach it better than he does. He taught Algorithms this semester and I wish I had taken it with him.

I highly suggest taking Calc 2 over the summer somewhere else. Maybe physics also if you decide to take it.
Nael Abu-Ghazaleh — overall rating 4.6/5 based on 34 ratings; average difficulty 3.3/5.

Professor Nael Abu-Ghazaleh — CS202 (RateMyProfessors review, 2016-03-10). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
He's super-helpful and he knows his field really well, but his content usually requires a strong background in the course he's taking. Be prepared to work really hard and learn a lot! i wont recommend taking his class if you aren't REALLY interested/dont have knowledge of systems. If you do, no one can do a better job at it.

Professor Nael Abu-Ghazaleh — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2014-05-14). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Nael is one of my favorite professors i've had at BU so far. Going to lecture helps a lot. Also do not be afraid to go to his office hours, he provides incredible help for the labs without giving away the answer. This class is the most informative CS course i've taken, and I finished the semester with a firm grasp of programming finally.

Professor Nael Abu-Ghazaleh — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2014-01-03). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 2.0/5.
Nael is an excellent professor. He knows his stuff, and is very detailed in his explanations. He is more than willing to dial back the time schedule if the class is struggling. Start the assignments on time and don't be afraid of a challenge. 220 is an excellent course and Nael teaches it very well. If you're scared, don't be. It's not bad.

Professor Nael Abu-Ghazaleh — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2013-12-18). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
This is one of the best professors for computer science. The class is hard but one of the most fair classes, if you work you get good grades and he's always there to help.

Professor Nael Abu-Ghazaleh — CS (general) (RateMyProfessors review, 2013-01-04). Student rating: quality 4.5/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
RECOMMENDED. Learned a lot from him. Labs can get difficult but very interesting and satisfying when completing. Lectures are important when going over complex ideas. Office hours are very helpful when you seek help. Very responsive to emails and questions as well.
Thomas Bartenstein — overall rating 2.7/5; 40% would take again; average difficulty 3.8/5.

Professor Thomas Bartenstein — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-12-20). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
Professor Bartenstein is such a sweet chill guy but I definitely see where his rmp ratings come from. His lectures can be a boring and tests are difficult. Make sure to compare homework assignments with other people because you can lose a lot of points there. Pop quizzes are random to check for attendance. The projects get increasingly difficult.

Professor Thomas Bartenstein — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-10-19). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
Extremely monotone and boring lectures, he mostly reads off the slides, so it's impossible to understand what's important or not. Pop quizzes are memorization-based, so it's hard to do well. He's slightly passive-aggressive when you ask questions via email, so it's hard to approach him. His exams are difficult and will tank your grade. Avoid him.

Professor Thomas Bartenstein — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-09-16). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
I took 220 in my sophomore year. His famous bomb project is quite entertaining if you had good TA, and makefile learnt in this class will be used in 310 and other higher level courses. Be prepared for pop quizzes (though they don't take much of the grades), and read syllabus closely as there's bonus question sometimes.

Professor Thomas Bartenstein — CS320 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-06-10). Student rating: quality 2.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
He's okay for 220, but isn't as good for this class. The class felt disorganized, and it seemed like the professor wasn't as well-versed in the material as he should be, which made it more difficult to learn. Example: there were no HW assignments between the first and second tests, which made it difficult to prepare for questions on the second test.

Professor Thomas Bartenstein — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-12-11). Student rating: quality 3.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
If you have to take him he's really not that bad. Lectures can be boring but just listen to what he says. Tests have gotten much easier over the years. Very accessible outside of class and very kind but not the best at clearing up confusing topics. Slides are very detailed for self-studying but attendance is mandatory because of pop quizzes.

Kenneth Chiu — overall rating 4.1/5 based on 27 ratings; 83% would take again; average difficulty 3.9/5. Teaches upper-level courses including deep learning / HPC (CS447), AI (CS440), and CS480-level electives.

Professor Kenneth Chiu — CS480E (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-11-02). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
this class is sooooo hard bruh

Professor Kenneth Chiu — CS447 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-05-23). Student rating: quality 3.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
I also had Ken for deep learning. Despite being around longer, this class felt far more disorganized. I do well with organized lectures and this was unfortunately the opposite of that. Additionally, assignments were very inconsistently posted which made it difficult to know when we would have work. Overall, prob wouldn't take HPC again.

Professor Kenneth Chiu — CS480E (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-12-22). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
We were graded only by attendance & 4 projects, all graded leniently. Professor Chiu keeps his lectures engaging & encourages participation. Projects are great for learning the material but are incredibly hard & time consuming. I wish they were smaller, as oftentimes we went months without applying the knowledge, which made retaining the info hard.

Professor Kenneth Chiu — CS480F (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-12-14). Student rating: quality 2.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
An alright professor that can get you by content-wise, lectures are particularly stale and require a level of self investment.

Professor Kenneth Chiu — CS440 (RateMyProfessors review, 2023-12-09). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Legend. Effective teacher. Grading breakdown is 47% 1-2 tests, 47% 3-4 programming assignments, 6% participation (+attendance) which is amazing imo. Quick responses on Piazza.
Leslie Lander — overall rating 3.7/5 based on 58 ratings; 71% would take again; average difficulty 2.3/5. Teaches design patterns (CS442/CS542); coordinates the 4+1 program.

Professor Leslie Lander — 4+1 program advising (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-08-31). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 2.0/5.
I didn't take this professor in a class but delt with him when entering the 4+1 program. It was extremely frustrating to deal with him. He would take weeks to reply and then repeat the same things we had talked about a day earlier. Long story short I had to have someone else help me even though he is the program coordinator. Extremely frustrating.

Professor Leslie Lander — CS442 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-12-19). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
Prof. Lander is chill and makes the course easy, with straightforward assignments and exams that mostly involve following instructions. However, the class felt too simple, so I had to self-teach a lot of the course as I wanted to learn more about design patterns. It's a good option if you want a light workload and more time for other activities.

Professor Leslie Lander — CS442 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-09-04). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
The course was straightforward and easy to earn an A. Lectures can be dull, but the assignments and tests mainly require following instructions, though they are interesting. The professor is kind and helpful. The TA was excellent, ensuring everyone understood the assignments, making announcements, and taking assignment demos. He was very helpful.

Professor Leslie Lander — CS542 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-08-24). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
It is unironically harder to fail the course than to get an A. Lectures are pretty dry at times, but assignments and tests boil down to 'can you follow simple instructions?' That being said, you can learn this entire course in a few hours through Scott Bain's PMI lectures, so only do this class in the summer or for an A. He's a great guy though!

Professor Leslie Lander — CS442 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-05-15). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
This class is incredibly easy, and Lander is extremely nice, but you won't learn a thing. Lectures are comprised of him following the textbook and writing code, but the assignments/quizzes/tests are so straightforward you don't use the concepts learned at all. Take this class only if you're looking for an easy A/free elective.
Mike Lewis — overall rating 4.0/5; 76% would take again; average difficulty 3.2/5. Department chair.

Professor Mike Lewis — CS310 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-05-23). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Exams were more tests of your english comprehension skills than of the actual content. Lectures are somewhat helpful and his teaching is okay, nothing super outstanding imo. Coding Projects and quizzes were light even without AI.

Professor Mike Lewis — CS310 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-12-12). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 2.0/5.
Breaks down the material and makes it easy to understand. Has a sense of humor, so class isn't always boring. He is the head of the CS department, so he knows the subject really well, but he is also extremely busy.

Professor Mike Lewis — CS350 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-02-06). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
Prof. Lewis is amazing!! Exams and projects are relatively difficult, but he makes up for that by curving at the end (depending on how bad overall scores are) and giving extra time for projects. Aside from that, he's a really caring professor and puts his students first.

Professor Mike Lewis — CS350 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-08-21). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
The course is 310, but he was solid. Projects are a HUGE part of your grade, so start them early and don't underestimate them. Use your TA/CA on projects and do decently well on the quizzes/exams, and you shouldn't have a problem getting a good grade.

Professor Mike Lewis — CS240 (RateMyProfessors review, 2023-12-16). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Lewis is the single most qualified teacher in this department. He only needed a board and markers (a prof. who doesn't depend on slides?). Lewis ensured all concepts and material were covered to only the extent we needed to know, and his explanations were incredibly solid.
Patrick Madden — overall rating 4.2/5 based on 64 ratings; 85% would take again; average difficulty 2.3/5.

Professor Patrick Madden — CS441 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-11-14). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
If you have the option to take a class with him, take it. He grades easily, and there are no surprises on tests and assignments.

Professor Patrick Madden — CS375 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-10-29). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Professor Madden sets the standard! His lectures are clear, easy to follow, and all recorded for your reference. His tests are very fair, and if you pay attention in class and use the study guide, you'll be set up for success. He genuinely wants his students to succeed!

Professor Patrick Madden — CS120 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-01-17). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
The exams are pretty hard and you aren't exactly prepared well for them. He is a fun guy, but he really just reads the slides. To be fair, the course is basically EE with minimal C coding, so it's already very hard to grasp. Best advice is to earn most points from homework and lab which are relatively mediocre, and try your best on exams.

Professor Patrick Madden — CS120 (RateMyProfessors review, 2023-05-24). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 2.0/5.
Overall great professor. Talked with me when I needed help. Had a great sense of humor. Great teacher of relatively complex ideas.

Professor Patrick Madden — CS120 (RateMyProfessors review, 2023-05-15). Student rating: quality 4.0/5, difficulty 2.0/5.
Madden is great, but not amazing. He explains things very well for the most part, and is super caring and helpful. He really wants his students to succeed. The reason I give him a 4/5 is because he spends too much time on easy topics, and too little time on difficult topics. Kinda strange. Overall, I definitely recommend him.
Ping Yang — overall rating 4.7/5 based on 17 ratings; 75% would take again; average difficulty 2.4/5.

Professor Ping Yang — CS558 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-12-17). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 2.0/5.
Prof. Yang is awesome! Her slides are very nicely made, and I appreciate that she uploads pre-recorded videos where she goes through lectures again. Each lecture is explained masterfully, and the assignments are very manageable. She gives lots of extra credit and is accessible outside of class. It's clear she knows her stuff and wants us to succeed.

Professor Ping Yang — CS558 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-03-26). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Professor Yang is nice and caring. The lectures are well delivered and there are also recordings to watch. Exams are fair, there are no tricky questions and you can definitely get a good score after practice.

Professor Ping Yang — CS558 (RateMyProfessors review, 2022-11-08). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Easy grade.

Professor Ping Yang — CS571 (RateMyProfessors review, 2020-05-26). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
Prof. Yang for CS 571 - Programming Languages is calm and inspirational. Her grading criteria is very transparent. All her lectures are delivered at a slow pace and might sometimes bore you, but is beneficial in terms of understanding basic concepts well. Many Programming languages are covered over the course along with assignments.

Professor Ping Yang — CS571 (RateMyProfessors review, 2019-04-18). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Lectures are very clear. Slides are good. Learned many different programming languages. Tests are not hard if you spent time working on the assignment and studying for the exam.
Adnan Rakin — overall rating 4.4/5 based on 9 ratings; 89% would take again; average difficulty 3.2/5. Teaches Machine Learning (CS436).

Professor Adnan Rakin — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-12-11). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
one of the best CS professors at Bing imo, hes the goat. exams felt hard (questions weren't always structured in a way that was done in class) but were graded fairly and hes willing to help during them. he cares about us understanding the material and hes an enthusiastic lecturer. overall good class, actually feel like i learned a lot.

Professor Adnan Rakin — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-02-05). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
The professor is very down-to-earth and highly knowledgeable in machine learning. They make the subject easier to understand with regular quizzes and reduce stress by offering extra credit, focusing on genuine learning rather than memorization. Highly recommended you will see the difference.

Professor Adnan Rakin — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-02-03). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
Professor Adnan Rakin is an exceptional instructor. Their Machine Learning class was one of the best I've taken, with clear and engaging explanations that made complex topics easy to understand. Their passion and teaching style set them apart, and I now appreciate their approach even more after taking other CS courses. Highly recommended!

Professor Adnan Rakin — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-01-23). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 4.0/5.
He is an Amazing Professor, really made the concepts easy to understand. The exams were very easy. But Beware! there are lots of assignments for you. Attending his office hours would be very beneficial for you during his classes.

Professor Adnan Rakin — CS220 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-06-05). Student rating: quality 5.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
he is a very good professor.
Sujoy Sikdar — overall rating 2.1/5 based on 16 ratings; 25% would take again; average difficulty 3.8/5. Teaches Machine Learning (CS436) and Algorithms (CS575).

Professor Sujoy Sikdar — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-04-05). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
Possibly the worst class I've taken in all 4 years here. Terribly long and difficult hws, pop quizzes nearly everyday that has questions scattered across the entire lecture, and not even gonna mention exams. Incredibly difficult to follow lecture material. Grades harshly. Avoid at all costs.

Professor Sujoy Sikdar — CS575 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-02-25). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
Poor pedagogy, real world implementation not taken into account, lecture is more of mathematical jargon but exam is of analytical implementation. Be aware of expecting stark difference in lecture & exams questions. If have prerequisite of ML take on own risk otherwise strictly avoid. Any free YouTube playlist is mush better than taking this course.

Professor Sujoy Sikdar — CS575 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-05-17). Student rating: quality 3.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
He's a relatively new professor so his lectures are a bit unpolished. He tries his level best to make sure the students get the basics of algorithms right. Although the focus on mathematical proofs is a bit too much in this class I guess it may help in developing a better understanding of the algorithms!

Professor Sujoy Sikdar — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2024-01-05). Student rating: quality 2.0/5, difficulty 3.0/5.
I was able to resubmit an unfinished assignment after finals which saved my grade. Very accessible outside of class, but he definitely needs to reformat how the material is taught because it was very hard to follow and understand unless you're laser-focused at each lecture.

Professor Sujoy Sikdar — CS436 (RateMyProfessors review, 2023-10-13). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
Jumps around between lecture slides, very difficult to follow and zero coherence. HWs are assigned erratically and can be assigned even a week before they are due, with each HW being very difficult. Attendance is mandatory, with proof that you were in class (no sign-in sheet, you must upload and send in your notes). ZERO respect for the student.
George Weinschenk — overall rating 3.0/5 based on 179 ratings; 32% would take again; average difficulty 3.2/5. Frequently teaches CS101.

Professor George Weinschenk — CS101 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-03-18). Student rating: quality 2.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
CS101 is an easy A but incredibly boring. Due to my section being at 7PM, I was very tired by the end of the lecture. Most of the lectures are done by guest speakers, with only a few actually being given by the professor. There is no reason to pay attention unless you are particularly interested in the guest speaker presentation.

Professor George Weinschenk — CS101 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-01-27). Student rating: quality 2.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
The most fatiguing class to sit through even though it's an easy A. Most lectures are filled with guest speakers in the computer science field, but can feel really boring since it's an evening class. Attendance is mandatory for passing the class and doing well—on top of him taking attendance at the start and end of each class.

Professor George Weinschenk — CS301 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-01-26). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 5.0/5.
A little late on writing this review but he is not a great professor. I assume they couldn't find another professor who wanted to teach this course which is why we all ended up with George. So much homework, focus is on grammar, not on CS.

Professor George Weinschenk — CS101 (RateMyProfessors review, 2026-01-23). Student rating: quality 1.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
this was a very very boring lecture that unfortunately all cs students will have to sit through. it felt more like a prison than a class and i learned nothing the whole semester. a waste of time.

Professor George Weinschenk — CS101 (RateMyProfessors review, 2025-12-12). Student rating: quality 2.0/5, difficulty 1.0/5.
CS101 is a drag and Weinschenk doesn't alleviate you from that reality.
